---
title:  
    "Reconstructing EXPIII Phylogeny based on Minority information"
date: 
    "February 13th, 2025"
author:  
    "Ali Movasati"  
output:  
    html_document:
        #css: style.css
        toc: true
        toc_depth: 2
        toc_float: true
        number_sections: true
        theme: lumen
        highlight: zenburn
        code_folding: hide
        code_download: true # to make the Rmd source code available
---




```{r loading libraries, echo=FALSE}

# loading libraries
library("ggplot2")
library("ggpubr")
library("plotly")
library("ggvenn")
library("hrbrthemes")
library("DT")
library("dplyr")
library("tidyr")
library("Biostrings")
library("stringr")
library("magrittr")
library("rstatix")
library("ape")

```


```{r read-in the data}

# determine the server path

if (file.exists("/home/amovas/")){
  print("Remote HPC Connection!")
  wd <- "/home/amovas/data/genome-evo-proj/"
} else {
  print("Local PC Connection!")
  wd <- "/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/hpc-research/genome-evo-proj/"
}

# load data
variants_ann <- read.table(file = paste0(wd, "results/tables/pipeline-outputs/all_annotated_variants.tsv.gz"), 
sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, header = TRUE)


# correct for the switched experimental lines
variants_ann$exp_line[variants_ann$exp_line == 15 & variants_ann$passage > 360] <- "A"
variants_ann$exp_line[variants_ann$exp_line == 16 & variants_ann$passage > 360] <- "B"

variants_ann$exp_line[variants_ann$exp_line == "A"] <- "16"
variants_ann$exp_line[variants_ann$exp_line == "B"] <- "15"

# determine what data to be included

exp <- "EXPIII"
end_psg <- 500

variants_ann_expiii <- variants_ann %>% filter(exp_line %in% 13:16,
        passage <= end_psg) 


rm(variants_ann)

# facors

host_factor <- c("MT2", "MT4")
exp_line_factor <- c("MT-2_1", "MT-2_2", "MT-4_1", "MT-4_2")

translational_impacts <- c("U", "S", "N")
gene_factor <- c("gag", "pol", "vif", "vpr", "tat", "rev", "vpu", "envelope", "nef")
feature_factor <- c("5utr", "gag", "pol", "vif", "vpr", "tat1", "rev1", "vpu", "envelope", "tat2", "rev2", "nef", "3utr")

# format the dataframe
variants_ann_expiii <- variants_ann_expiii %>%
                            mutate(host_line = ifelse(exp_line %in% c("13", "14"), "MT2", "MT4"), # set host cell line info
                            exp_line = case_when(exp_line==13 ~ "MT-2_1", exp_line==14 ~ "MT-2_2", exp_line==15 ~ "MT-4_1", exp_line==16 ~ "MT-4_2"), # update exp line labels
                            mut_info = paste(genomic_pos, ref_allele, alt_allele, sep = "_")) %>% 
                            mutate(effect_simplified = case_when(effect == "synonymous_variant" ~ "S", effect == "untranslated_region" ~ "U")) %>%
                            mutate(effect_simplified = replace_na(effect_simplified, "N")) %>%
                            mutate(passage = as.factor(passage),
                            exp_line = factor(exp_line, levels = exp_line_factor),
                            host_line = factor(host_line, levels = host_factor),
                            effect_simplified = factor(effect_simplified, levels = translational_impacts)) 

# remove duplicate records for the same variants
variants_expiii <- variants_ann_expiii %>% filter(!duplicated(variant_id))

# Visual settings

line_palette <- c("#ff00ff", "#ff2400", "#6600cc", "#0000ff")
breaks_step <- 50
line_labels <- c("MT-2_1", "MT-2_2", "MT-4_1", "MT-4_2")


```



```{r}




df <- variants_expiii_subset %>% 
                filter(passage %in% seq(10,500,10) & allele_freq >= 0.05 & coverage >= 1000 & mut_type == "P") %>%
                select(c(genomic_pos, ref_allele, alt_allele, allele_freq, exp_line, passage)) %>%
                mutate(
    mutation_type = case_when(
        (ref_allele %in% purines & alt_allele %in% purines) |
        (ref_allele %in% pyrimidines & alt_allele %in% pyrimidines) ~ "Transition",
        TRUE ~ "Transversion")
    )


# Step 1: Create a unique population identifier
df <- df %>%
  mutate(population = paste(exp_line, passage, sep = "_")) 

# Step 2: Reshape data to wide format
df_wide <- df %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(genomic_pos, alt_allele, mutation_type),  # Ensures each row is unique to a genomic position and allele
    names_from = population, 
    values_from = allele_freq, 
    values_fill = list(allele_freq = 0)
  )

# Step 3: Compute Distance Matrix
populations <- colnames(df_wide)[-(1:3)]  # Remove genomic_pos & alt_allele columns
num_pops <- length(populations)

# Initialize distance matrix
distance_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = num_pops, ncol = num_pops, dimnames = list(populations, populations))

# Compute absolute frequency differences across positions
for (i in 1:num_pops) {
  for (j in i:num_pops) {
    pop_i_tr <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transition",][[populations[i]]]
    pop_j_tr <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transition",][[populations[j]]]
    
    pop_i_tv <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transversion",][[populations[i]]]
    pop_j_tv <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transversion",][[populations[j]]]
    
    # Compute absolute differences, summing over all positions
    distance_matrix[i, j] <- sum(c(abs(pop_i_tr - pop_j_tr), abs(pop_i_tv - pop_j_tv)), na.rm = TRUE)
  }
}


# Make the matrix symmetric
distance_matrix[lower.tri(distance_matrix)] <- t(distance_matrix)[lower.tri(distance_matrix)]



# Step 4: Convert to Distance Object
distance_obj <- as.dist(distance_matrix)

# Step 5: Perform Neighbor-Joining Phylogeny
nj_tree <- nj(distance_obj)

# Define colors for each lineage/population
color_map <- c(
  "MT-2_1" = "#ff00ff",   # Color for MT-4_1
  "MT-2_2" = "#ff2400", # Color for MT-4_2
  "MT-4_1" = "#6600cc",  # Color for MT-2_1
  "MT-4_2" = "#0000ff" # Color for MT-2_2
)

# Assign colors based on population (assuming tip labels are the populations)
node_colors <- sapply(nj_tree$tip.label, function(x) {
  # Extract population info from the tip label (assuming labels are like "MT-2_1")
  if (grepl("MT-4_1", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-4_1"])
  } else if (grepl("MT-4_2", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-4_2"])
  } else if (grepl("MT-2_1", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-2_1"])
  } else if (grepl("MT-2_2", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-2_2"])
  } else {
    return("black")  # Default color for unknown patterns
  }
})


plot(nj_tree, main = "Neighbor-Joining Tree of Non-Randomized Majority Variants", cex = 0.6, tip.color = node_colors)


# save figure
date <- str_split(Sys.time(), pattern = " ")[[1]][1]
fig_dir <- paste0(wd, "results/figs/publication/expiii/provisional/", date, "/")
ifelse(!dir.exists(file.path(fig_dir)), dir.create(file.path(fig_dir)), FALSE)
pdf(file = paste0(fig_dir, "non_neutral_phylo.pdf"), height = 14, width = 14)
plot(nj_tree, main = "Neighbor-Joining Tree of Non-Randomized Majority Variants", cex = 0.6, tip.color = node_colors)
dev.off()
pdf(file = "/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/research/manuscripts/exp-2e-update-manuscript/figures/non_neutral_phylo.pdf", height = 14, width = 14)
plot(nj_tree, main = "Neighbor-Joining Tree of Randomized Majority Variants", cex = 0.6, tip.color = node_colors)
dev.off()


# Save the phylogenetic tree in Newick format
write.tree(nj_tree, file = "/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/research/manuscripts/exp-2e-update-manuscript/figures/non_neutral_phylogenetic_tree.nwk")



```


```{r}


# read in ancestral reference sequence 
fastaFile <- readDNAStringSet("/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/hpc-research/genome-evo-proj/data/reference/plasmid/plasmid-consensus/hiv_plasmid_consensus_genome.fasta")
seq_name = names(fastaFile)
sequence = paste(fastaFile)
sequence <- strsplit(sequence, NULL)[[1]]
sequence <- sequence[454:9625]



variants_expiii_subset <- variants_expiii %>% 
        filter(allele_freq >= 0.5 & coverage >= 1000 & mut_type == "P")


shuffler <- function(x){
    pos <- str_split(x, "_")[[1]][1]
    ref <- str_split(x, "_")[[1]][2]
    alt <- str_split(x, "_")[[1]][3]
    new_pos <- sample(which(sequence == "G"), 1)
    return(paste(new_pos, ref, alt, sep = "_"))
}

for (exp_line in line_labels){
    set <- variants_expiii_subset %>% 
        filter(exp_line == exp_line) %>%
        select(mut_info) %>%
        distinct() %>%
        pull()
    
    randomized_set <- sapply(set, shuffler)

    variants_expiii_subset$new_mut_info[variants_expiii_subset$exp_line == exp_line] <- randomized_set[variants_expiii_subset$mut_info[variants_expiii_subset$exp_line == exp_line]]

    variants_expiii_subset$genomic_pos[variants_expiii_subset$exp_line == exp_line] <- sapply(str_split(variants_expiii_subset$new_mut_info[variants_expiii_subset$exp_line == exp_line], "_"), "[[",1)


}



```



```{r}




df <- variants_expiii_subset %>% 
                filter(passage %in% seq(10,500,10) & allele_freq >= 0.05 & coverage >= 1000 & mut_type == "P") %>%
                select(c(genomic_pos, ref_allele, alt_allele, allele_freq, exp_line, passage)) %>%
                mutate(
    mutation_type = case_when(
        (ref_allele %in% purines & alt_allele %in% purines) |
        (ref_allele %in% pyrimidines & alt_allele %in% pyrimidines) ~ "Transition",
        TRUE ~ "Transversion")
    )


# Step 1: Create a unique population identifier
df <- df %>%
  mutate(population = paste(exp_line, passage, sep = "_")) 

# Step 2: Reshape data to wide format
df_wide <- df %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(genomic_pos, alt_allele, mutation_type),  # Ensures each row is unique to a genomic position and allele
    names_from = population, 
    values_from = allele_freq, 
    values_fill = list(allele_freq = 0)
  )

# Step 3: Compute Distance Matrix
populations <- colnames(df_wide)[-(1:3)]  # Remove genomic_pos & alt_allele columns
num_pops <- length(populations)

# Initialize distance matrix
distance_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = num_pops, ncol = num_pops, dimnames = list(populations, populations))

# Compute absolute frequency differences across positions
for (i in 1:num_pops) {
  for (j in i:num_pops) {
    pop_i_tr <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transition",][[populations[i]]]
    pop_j_tr <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transition",][[populations[j]]]
    
    pop_i_tv <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transversion",][[populations[i]]]
    pop_j_tv <- df_wide[df_wide$mutation_type == "Transversion",][[populations[j]]]
    
    # Compute absolute differences, summing over all positions
    distance_matrix[i, j] <- sum(c(abs(pop_i_tr - pop_j_tr), abs(pop_i_tv - pop_j_tv)), na.rm = TRUE)
  }
}


# Make the matrix symmetric
distance_matrix[lower.tri(distance_matrix)] <- t(distance_matrix)[lower.tri(distance_matrix)]



# Step 4: Convert to Distance Object
distance_obj <- as.dist(distance_matrix)

# Step 5: Perform Neighbor-Joining Phylogeny
nj_tree <- nj(distance_obj)

# Define colors for each lineage/population
color_map <- c(
  "MT-2_1" = "#ff00ff",   # Color for MT-4_1
  "MT-2_2" = "#ff2400", # Color for MT-4_2
  "MT-4_1" = "#6600cc",  # Color for MT-2_1
  "MT-4_2" = "#0000ff" # Color for MT-2_2
)

# Assign colors based on population (assuming tip labels are the populations)
node_colors <- sapply(nj_tree$tip.label, function(x) {
  # Extract population info from the tip label (assuming labels are like "MT-2_1")
  if (grepl("MT-4_1", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-4_1"])
  } else if (grepl("MT-4_2", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-4_2"])
  } else if (grepl("MT-2_1", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-2_1"])
  } else if (grepl("MT-2_2", x)) {
    return(color_map["MT-2_2"])
  } else {
    return("black")  # Default color for unknown patterns
  }
})


plot(nj_tree, main = "Neighbor-Joining Tree of Randomized Majority Variants", cex = 0.6, tip.color = node_colors)


# save figure
date <- str_split(Sys.time(), pattern = " ")[[1]][1]
fig_dir <- paste0(wd, "results/figs/publication/expiii/provisional/", date, "/")
ifelse(!dir.exists(file.path(fig_dir)), dir.create(file.path(fig_dir)), FALSE)
pdf(file = paste0(fig_dir, "neutral_phylo.pdf"), height = 14, width = 14)
plot(nj_tree, main = "Neighbor-Joining Tree of Randomized Majority Variants", cex = 0.6, tip.color = node_colors)
dev.off()
pdf(file = "/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/research/manuscripts/exp-2e-update-manuscript/figures/neutral_phylo.pdf", height = 14, width = 14)
plot(nj_tree, main = "Neighbor-Joining Tree of Randomized Majority Variants", cex = 0.6, tip.color = node_colors)
dev.off()


# Save the phylogenetic tree in Newick format
write.tree(nj_tree, file = "/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/research/manuscripts/exp-2e-update-manuscript/figures/neutral_phylogenetic_tree.nwk")



```
